COURSE WORKBOOK
with Gretta Van Riel, Rob Ward, Chase Dimond
Richard Li, Nick Shackelford
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 4 --

In this lesson, I’ll teach you my tried and tested
method for finding quality suppliers. As I mentioned
earlier, scaling manufacturing not only involves
making larger quantities of product but also
producing at a higher quality. And at the end of the
day quality products mean more positive feedback
and a higher Customer Lifetime Value (CLV).
The Best Suppliers:
• 	Scale with your business
• 	Match your quality standards
• 	Help you grow your business
Product Sourcing
Method #1: Sourcing In-House
The FBI Method
Investigating your competitors’ suppliers
Step 1: Identify your competitor.
Step 2: Go on their website and check for their
company name.
Step 3: Plug in the company name on JungleScout.
Step 3: Choose a manufacturer under “Top
Suppliers.”
Method #2: External Sourcing Agent
Pros
• 	Available to visit many suppliers
Cons
• 	Limited supplier relationships
• 	May take a cut of the supplier’s fee (5-10%)
LESSON:
SOURCING QUALITY
SUPPLIERS
MODULE:
MANUFACTURING &
OPERATIONS AT SCALE
ACTION ITEMS
❏ 	Identify your direct competitors.
❏ 	Find your top competitors’ suppliers.
❏ 	Cold call those suppliers using my scripts.

Cold Calling Scripts
Use the cold call script templates below to call your competitor’s suppliers.
The Business Card Approach:
Hi, my name is [Name] and I’m from an [Company, Description]. I met you guys at [Event/
Date] and someone gave me a business card of yours. I’d like to speak to someone who
looks after the [Your Country] market.
The Network Connection:
Hi, my name is [Name] and I’m from an [Company, Description]. I met you guys at [Event/
Date] and [Make Up a Name] recommended we reach out to you. I’d like to speak to
someone who looks after the [Your Country] market.
